pr.title: Code review fixes: Cypher bug, version pin, idempotent seeds, docs
pr.body: ## Summary Self-critique review of the `context-graph` branch identified four issues: 1. **`graph_utils.py` — Cypher query bug**: `get_subgraph()` referenced an undefined variable `r` in its RETURN clause, which would throw a Neo4j query error at runtime. Fixed to use `reduce(acc = [], rels IN collect(relationships(p)) | acc + rels)` to correctly flatten and return path relationships. 2. **`requirements.txt` — version pin too low**: `langchain>=0.1.0` allows installing LangChain 0.x which does NOT have the `create_agent` function used in `langchain_agent.py`. Bumped to `langchain>=1.0.0`. 3. **`init_sample_data.py` — non-idempotent seed script**: The script used `CREATE` for nodes but also defined a `unique_node_name` constraint, meaning a second run would always fail. Changed all node/relationship creation to `MERGE` + `SET` (matching the pattern already used in `init_healthcare_data.py`). 4. **`SETUP_SUMMARY.md` — stale/incorrect documentation**: Referenced a non-existent `.claude/skills/` directory, listed "OpenAI API key" as prerequisite (should be Gemini), and described 4 agent tools that don't exist in the actual `langchain_agent.py` (which has 3 memory tools). Updated to match reality. ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] Verify `get_subgraph()` returns expected nodes + relationships against a live Neo4j instance — the `reduce()` flattening of relationship paths is correct Cypher but worth a manual spot check - [ ] Confirm `pip install -r requirements.txt` resolves `langchain>=1.0.0` correctly in a clean venv - [ ] Run `python init_sample_data.py` twice against a fresh Neo4j — second run should succeed without errors ### Notes - The `context-graph` branch is an orphan (no shared history with `main`), so this PR targets `context-graph` directly. - All Python files compile and import cleanly; no type checker or linter config exists in the repo so validation was done via `py_compile` and runtime import checks. Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/9abac372166b4ba496d932ba4d5f56bc Requested by: @pthebesfsu-a11y ---
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
